description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped stone with a dark, almost black coloration and a rough, matte surface. The stone has a wedge-like profile, with one end tapering to a thinner edge and the opposite end broader and more angular. Subtle mottling and lighter patches are visible across its surface, suggesting natural mineral variation. One side features a hand-painted white marking, possibly an inventory or catalog number, indicating prior handling or collection. The stone's surface is uneven, with small pits and minor chips, giving it a weathered, natural appearance. Its overall form and finish suggest it could be found in urban landscaping or as a loose obstacle on a sidewalk or park path.
description: A dark, wedge-shaped stone with a rough, matte surface and visible natural mottling. One side bears a white painted marking, and the stone appears weathered and irregular.
description_view_0: The stone appears very thin and elongated from this edge-on view, highlighting its tapered profile.
description_view_1: This side shows the broadest face, with visible white painted markings and mottled surface texture.
description_view_2: Another edge-on view, similar to view 0, emphasizing the stone's narrow thickness and elongated shape.
description_view_3: The opposite broad face, lacking markings, with a rough, uneven surface and visible natural pitting.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or loose obstacle on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular mottling and mineral variation, with a hand-painted white marking on one side; no regular or artificial patterns.
attribute_weathering: Surface is rough and pitted, with minor chips and a generally weathered appearance, indicating moderate exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Typically rests on soil, gravel, or pavement, visually blending with other natural stones but contrasting with smoother man-made surfaces.
